# Introduction

## Purpose

This document describes the software requirements for the Agent Management feature within the "AI-Agent for Virtualizing Enterprise Operation" project. This feature enables users to create, configure, and manage AI agents that represent virtual employees operating within their workspace.

## Scope

The Agent Management subsystem provides an interface for users to carry out the full lifecycle of an agent: from creation and behavior configuration, to enabling/disabling activity, and permanent deletion. Each agent is stored as a dedicated directory within the workspace, containing a skill.md file that defines its behavior.

## Definitions & Abbreviations

| **Term**         | **Definition**                                                                          |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Agent**        | An AI virtual employee configured and operating within a workspace                      |
| **Workspace**    | A dedicated **OpenClaw** instance provisioned for a user or organization                |
| **skill.md**     | A Markdown file that defines the behavior, constraints, and instructions for an agent   |
| **Model**        | The AI language model the agent uses to process tasks                                   |
| **Role**         | The job title or function of the agent within the workspace (e.g., Developer, Designer) |
| **Instruction**  | Behavioral guidance embedded into the agent's skill.md file                             |
| **Agent Status** | ACTIVE - currently operational; INACTIVE - suspended but retained                       |

# System Overview

## System Context

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## User Roles & Permissions

User roles are defined and managed by Feature 8 (Workspace User Management). The table below describes each role's permissions with respect to the Agent Management feature only.

| **User Role**         | **Agent Management Permissions**                                        |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Admin (workspace)** | Full access: view, create, edit, enable/disable, and delete agents      |
| **Editor / Member**   | View, create, edit, and enable/disable agents; **cannot** delete agents |
| **Viewer**            | View agent list and details only; no editing permitted                  |
| **Unauthenticated**   | No access; redirected to the login page                                 |

## Assumptions & Dependencies

- Users must be authenticated and have an active workspace (subscription status ACTIVE) before managing agents.
- The maximum number of agents per workspace depends on the subscription tier (Standard: up to 5 agents; Premium: up to 20 agents).
- Each agent corresponds to a dedicated directory within the workspace file system.
- The content of skill.md determines how the agent responds to and processes tasks.

# Functional Requirements

## FR-3.1 - View Agent List

| **Field**          | **Details**                                                                                                      |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Requirement ID** | FR-3.1                                                                                                           |
| **Name**           | View Agent List                                                                                                  |
| **Description**    | The system displays all agents currently existing within the user's workspace, along with their key information. |
| **Input**          | User navigates to the Agent Management page                                                                      |
| **Processing**     | The system queries the database for all agents belonging to the user's workspace                                 |
| **Output**         | A list of agents with the following attributes: name, role, model in use, status (ACTIVE / INACTIVE)             |
| **Priority**       | High                                                                                                             |
| **Preconditions**  | User is authenticated and has an active workspace                                                                |

Information displayed per agent:

| **Attribute**    | **Description**                                        |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Agent Name**   | The display identifier of the agent                    |
| **Role**         | The agent's function or job title within the workspace |
| **Model**        | The AI model currently in use                          |
| **Status**       | ACTIVE or INACTIVE                                     |
| **Created Date** | Timestamp of when the agent was initially created      |

## FR-3.2 - Create New Agent

| **Field**          | **Details**                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Requirement ID** | FR-3.2                                                                                                                                                                          |
| **Name**           | Create New Agent                                                                                                                                                                |
| **Description**    | The system allows users to create a new agent by completing a configuration form.                                                                                               |
| **Input**          | User fills in the agent creation form: name, role, model, and instruction content                                                                                               |
| **Processing**     | The system creates an agent record in the database, provisions a dedicated directory within the workspace, and generates a skill.md file from the submitted instruction content |
| **Output**         | Agent is created successfully with an ACTIVE status; the user is redirected to the agent detail page                                                                            |
| **Priority**       | High                                                                                                                                                                            |
| **Preconditions**  | User has an active workspace; the current number of agents has not reached the subscription plan limit                                                                          |

Agent creation flow:

- User clicks "Create New Agent."
- System presents the agent configuration form.
- User enters a name, selects a role, selects an AI model, and enters instruction content.
- User clicks "Confirm."
- System validates the agent count against the subscription plan limit.
- System creates the agent record, provisions a /{agent-name}/ directory in the workspace, and writes the instruction content to skill.md.
- The new agent appears in the list with an ACTIVE status.

Business Rules**:**

- Agent names must be unique within the workspace.
- Agent names may only contain Latin letters (a-z, A-Z), digits (0-9), and hyphens (-). Spaces, Unicode characters (including accented letters, CJK characters, emoji, etc.), and other special characters are not permitted. This constraint ensures cross-platform file system compatibility and prevents encoding errors.
- If the agent limit has been reached, the system must display an informative message and prompt the user to upgrade their subscription plan.

## FR-3.3 - View Agent Details & Edit Agent

| **Field**          | **Details**                                                                                                                     |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Requirement ID** | FR-3.3                                                                                                                          |
| **Name**           | View Agent Details & Edit Agent                                                                                                 |
| **Description**    | The system allows users to view the full configuration of an agent and modify its settings.                                     |
| **Input**          | User clicks on an agent in the list                                                                                             |
| **Processing**     | System retrieves and displays the agent's full details; upon saving, updates the database record and rewrites the skill.md file |
| **Output**         | Agent detail page displaying all configuration fields; a success notification upon saving                                       |
| **Priority**       | High                                                                                                                            |
| **Preconditions**  | The agent exists within the user's workspace                                                                                    |

Editable fields:

| **Field**       | **Notes**                                                      |
| --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Agent Name**  | Can be renamed; the workspace directory is renamed accordingly |
| **Role**        | Updates the agent's job title                                  |
| **Model**       | Changes the AI model in use                                    |
| **Instruction** | Updated instruction content is written back to skill.md        |

Business Rules:

When a user renames an agent, the system must rename the corresponding workspace directory atomically, within the same transaction as the database update. If the directory rename operation fails, the entire change must be rolled back to prevent data inconsistency between the agent record and the skill.md file.

## FR-3.4 - Enable / Disable Agent

| **Field**          | **Details**                                                                                      |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Requirement ID** | FR-3.4                                                                                           |
| **Name**           | Enable / Disable Agent                                                                           |
| **Description**    | The system allows users to toggle the operational status of an agent without deleting any data.  |
| **Input**          | User clicks the enable/disable toggle on the agent card or the agent detail page                 |
| **Processing**     | System updates the agent's status to ACTIVE or INACTIVE in the database                          |
| **Output**         | Agent status is reflected immediately on the interface; a confirmation notification is displayed |
| **Priority**       | High                                                                                             |

Business Rules:

- An INACTIVE agent does not participate in task processing but retains all its configuration data and skill.md file.
- Users may re-enable an INACTIVE agent at any time.

## FR-3.5 - Delete Agent

| **Field**          | **Details**                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Requirement ID** | FR-3.5                                                                                                                                  |
| **Name**           | Delete Agent                                                                                                                            |
| **Description**    | The system allows users with the appropriate permissions to permanently delete an agent from the workspace.                             |
| **Input**          | User clicks "Delete" and confirms the action in a confirmation dialog                                                                   |
| **Processing**     | System removes the agent record from the database and deletes the agent's directory (including skill.md) from the workspace file system |
| **Output**         | Agent is removed from the list; a deletion success notification is displayed                                                            |
| **Priority**       | Medium                                                                                                                                  |

Business Rules:

- The system must present a confirmation dialog before executing the deletion.
- Only users with the Admin role may perform agent deletion (see Section 2.2).
- Deletion is permanent and cannot be undone.

# Non-Functional Requirements

| **ID**  | **Category** | **Requirement**                                                                                                            |
| ------- | ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| NFR-3.1 | Security     | Users may only view and manage agents belonging to their own workspace; cross-workspace access to agents is not permitted. |
| NFR-3.2 | Security     | All create, edit, and delete operations require an authenticated user session.                                             |
| NFR-3.3 | Performance  | The agent list page must load within 2 seconds under normal operating conditions.                                          |
| NFR-3.4 | Usability    | The end-to-end flow for creating a new agent must be completable within 5 user interactions.                               |
| NFR-3.5 | Consistency  | The agent status displayed on the UI must accurately reflect the actual status stored in the system at all times.          |

# Constraints

| **ID** | **Constraint**                                                                                                                                         |
| ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| C-1    | Users must have an active workspace (subscription status ACTIVE) to access Agent Management features.                                                  |
| C-2    | The maximum number of agents per workspace is determined by the subscription plan: Standard supports up to 5 agents; Premium supports up to 20 agents. |
| C-3    | Agent names must be unique within a workspace.                                                                                                         |
| C-4    | Agent names are restricted to Latin letters (a-z, A-Z), digits (0-9), and hyphens (-) to ensure file system compatibility.                             |
| C-5    | Agent deletion is a permanent, irreversible action.                                                                                                    |
